title: Deciphering the regulatory code of RNA inosine through enzymatic precision mapping and explainable deep learning model
author: shaoqing, han
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Adenosine-to-inosine (A-to-I) RNA editing is one of the most abundant RNA modifications, participating in multiple critical biological processes. Leveraging the high-confidence A-to-I editing sites identified by REX-seq, we developed InoDREAM (Inosine Deep Residual Enhanced Attention Model), an interpretable deep learning framework. This model successfully predicted multiple regulatory proteins exhibiting specific binding affinity to A-to-I edited transcripts, while simultaneously identifying disease-associated mutation sites and relevant cis-regulatory elements functionally linked to RNA editing events.
steps_raw: Adenosine-to-inosine (A-to-I) RNA editing is one of the most abundant RNA modifications, participating in multiple critical biological processes. Leveraging the high-confidence A-to-I editing sites identified by REX-seq, we developed InoDREAM (Inosine Deep Residual Enhanced Attention Model), an interpretable deep learning framework. This model successfully predicted multiple regulatory proteins exhibiting specific binding affinity to A-to-I edited transcripts, while simultaneously identifying disease-associated mutation sites and relevant cis-regulatory elements functionally linked to RNA editing events.
